other_observers: Tina Jones; found and identified by me
description: the bird was a tiny goose, much smaller (at most half the size) of nearby Greater Canada Geese. The overall shape was more stocky, mostly due to a relatively shorter neck. The head shape was somwhat blocky, though the head also looked relatively smaller in comparison to Canada Goose. The bill was black, and relatively smaller in comparison to the head than nearby geese. The eye was dark, as were the legs.  The bird's plumage was similar to nearby Canada Geese, with the exception of a thin white neck band at the base of the neck, and overall a much darker color. This was especially evident on the breast, which was extremely dark, almost a glossy dark brownish-purple.Behaviors: the bird was hanging out with a flock of Canada Geese on a cemetery lawn, and was very shy, much more so than the Canada GeeseCall: none heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer
